faqs.a: Group therapy is a form of psychotherapy where one or two therapists work with several people at the same time, usually somewhere between five and twelve members. The group meets on a regular schedule to talk through problems, practice new skills, and get feedback.

faqs.q: How is group therapy different from a support group?
faqs.a: Support groups are usually peer-led and free, while group therapy is run by a licensed clinician and is a formal treatment. Group therapy also isn't a place where members are forced to share before they're ready.

faqs.q: Is group therapy as effective as individual therapy?
faqs.a: It isn't a sign that someone's problem is less serious. For many conditions, group formats are a well-studied, first-line option, and group therapy can be used on its own or alongside individual therapy and medication.
